edge: Generic algorithms were obtained in previous work on user-level differential privacy to work for various learning tasks.
The focus of previous work was on the example-rich regime where users have many examples, while in this work, the example-scarce regime is considered where users only have a few examples.
A generic transformation of any item-level DP algorithm to a user-level DP algorithm is provided in this work.
A simple technique for adapting the exponential mechanism to the user-level setting is presented for pure-DP.

node_2: model's leakage of personal user information
edge: Differential privacy is a widely popular notion quantifying a model's leakage of personal user information.
Private machine learning algorithms have been devised under the assumption of item-level DP, where each user contributes a single example to the training dataset.
In the user-level DP setting, the algorithm's output needs to remain statistically indistinguishable even when all items belonging to a single user are substituted.
The algorithm needs to meet a strong privacy requirement where the output remains statistically indistinguishable even with substitution of samples from a single user.
Meeting the privacy requirement poses a challenge as the algorithm attempts to take advantage of the increased number of examples provided by users.
